title: Aerobic Exercise and Brain Health
content_html:
Aerobic Exercise and Brain Health

The disease-fighting, weight-controlling benefits of physical exercise, especially aerobic exercise, have long been known. Now, researchers have discovered another advantage: Physical exercise has a powerful effect on brain health, and the benefits go beyond the release of endorphins, the chemical in the brain that improves mood. Exercise affects the brain's plasticity—that is, its ability to reorganize itself—and can reduce the age-associated loss of brain tissue that decreases cognition in the elderly and in those who have disorders such as Alzheimer's disease.

Recent studies have found that exercise activates a number of factors in the brain, including a protein known as brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF), that stimulate the growth and development of brain cells. BDNF regulates the production of synapses, the connections between neurons that are essential for transmitting signals from one nerve cell to the next, and may also be involved in producing new nerve cells. Using rodent models, researchers found increased concentrations of BDNF in the hippocampus, an area of the brain involved in learning and memory and associated with dementia, after only one week of regular exercise. A study in older humans found a correlation between aerobic fitness, the size of the hippocampus, and performance on spatial memory tests. Other human studies noted that aerobic exercise increased the volume of gray matter in some parts of the brain.

Regular exercise can help stave off some effects of normal aging and delay or diminish the gravity of conditions such as Alzheimer's disease, depression, and multiple sclerosis. Even over a relatively short time, exercise can repair some of the loss in brain capacity associated with aging. The greatest effects have been found in processes such as decision-making. Aerobic exercise can also improve short-term memory in the elderly. Exercise has been found to lower the risk of Alzheimer's disease in mice by decreasing the buildup of a protein known as beta-amyloid, which forms the brain plaques that precede Alzheimer's. The mice also outperformed nonexercising mice in a memory test. In a study of multiple sclerosis patients, those who exercised regularly fared better than those who exercised less. The exercise group scored better on tests of cognitive function, and their brain scans showed less deterioration and more gray matter.

In addition to increasing brainpower, exercise can help relieve depression. Although it is well known that endorphins help relieve stress and reduce anxiety and depression, BDNF plays a role as well. Human studies have shown that people who have received a diagnosis of major depression typically have lower concentrations of BDNF in their blood. Animal studies indicate that corticosteroids, which the body produces in response to stress, decrease the availability of BDNF in the hippocampus. Exercise can counteract this effect. Exercise also lessens depression by increasing blood flow to the brain.

The link between aerobic exercise and improved brain function in the elderly and in people with impaired cognition could lead to new ways to prevent and treat brain disorders. Meanwhile, people may have more control over their own brain health than was previously believed.

questions.text: Exercise helps people feel good mentally because it releases endorphins, which put people in a better ...........
options: Increases brain plasticity and reduces age-associated loss of brain tissue
Lowers the risk of Alzheimer's disease
Improves short-term memory in the elderly
questions.question: Exercise helps people feel good mentally because it releases endorphins, which put people in a better ...........
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 1–8

Complete the notes below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
endorphins
better

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 1:

"endorphins, the chemical in the brain that improves mood"

Bước 3: Phân tích

improves mood = làm tâm trạng tốt hơn

Paraphrase:

put people in a better mood = khiến mọi người có tâm trạng tốt hơn

Đáp án: mood

_blockConfig.noteContent:
1.  Exercise helps people feel good mentally because it releases endorphins, which put people in a better [1]
2.  BDNF improves the connections between nerve cells in the brain because it [2] how those connections, or synapses, are made.
3.  Studies on rodents showed that there were larger [3] of BDNF in the brain after just one week of exercise.
4.  Exercise may lessen the [4] of Alzheimer's disease and other disorders that affect the brain.
5.  As people age, they may not function as well because they lose some brain [5], but exercise can repair some of this lost ability.
6.  A study with multiple sclerosis patients showed that those who exercised more had less [6] of the brain.
7.  Usually, smaller amounts of BDNF are found in the blood of people [7] with depression.
8.  Exercise may lessen the effects of stress because it can [8] the effects of corticosteroids, which are produced by stress.

questions.questionHtml:
1. Exercise helps people feel good mentally because it releases endorphins, which put people in a better 1_______

2. BDNF improves the connections between nerve cells in the brain because it 2_______ how those connections, or synapses, are made.

3. Studies on rodents showed that there were larger 3_______ of BDNF in the brain after just one week of exercise.

4. Exercise may lessen the 4_______ of Alzheimer's disease and other disorders that affect the brain.

5. As people age, they may not function as well because they lose some brain 5_______, but exercise can repair some of this lost ability.

6. A study with multiple sclerosis patients showed that those who exercised more had less 6_______ of the brain.

7. Usually, smaller amounts of BDNF are found in the blood of people 7_______ with depression.

8. Exercise may lessen the effects of stress because it can 8_______ the effects of corticosteroids, which are produced by stress.

questions.text: BDNF improves the connections between nerve cells in the brain because it .......... how those connections, or synapses, are made.
questions.question: BDNF improves the connections between nerve cells in the brain because it .......... how those connections, or synapses, are made.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
BDNF
synapses

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 2:

"BDNF regulates the production of synapses"

Bước 3: Phân tích

question:

how those connections, or synapses, are made

Bài đọc:

regulates the production of synapses

Paraphrase:

production = how something is made
 Đáp án: regulates

questions.text: Studies on rodents showed that there were larger .......... of BDNF in the brain after just one week of exercise.
questions.question: Studies on rodents showed that there were larger .......... of BDNF in the brain after just one week of exercise.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
rodents
one week

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 2:

"researchers found increased concentrations of BDNF ... after only one week of regular exercise"

Bước 3: Phân tích

larger = increased

Paraphrase:

larger concentrations = increased concentrations
 Đáp án: concentrations

questions.text: Exercise may lessen the .......... of Alzheimer's disease and other disorders that affect the brain.
options: Decreased growth and development of brain cells
questions.question: Exercise may lessen the .......... of Alzheimer's disease and other disorders that affect the brain.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
Alzheimer's disease
lessen

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 3:

"delay or diminish the gravity of conditions such as Alzheimer's disease"

Bước 3: Phân tích

lessen = diminish

Paraphrase:

lessen Alzheimer's disease = diminish the gravity of Alzheimer's disease

Đáp án: gravity

questions.text: As people age, they may not function as well because they lose some brain .........., but exercise can repair some of this lost ability.
questions.question: As people age, they may not function as well because they lose some brain .........., but exercise can repair some of this lost ability.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
aging
repair

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 3:

"exercise can repair some of the loss in brain capacity associated with aging"

Bước 3: Phân tích

question:

lose some brain ability

text:

loss in brain capacity

Paraphrase:
capacity = ability
Đáp án: capacity

questions.text: A study with multiple sclerosis patients showed that those who exercised more had less .......... of the brain.
options: Increased blood flow to the brain
questions.question: A study with multiple sclerosis patients showed that those who exercised more had less .......... of the brain.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
multiple sclerosis
brain

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 3:

"their brain scans showed less deterioration and more gray matter"

Bước 3: Phân tích

question:

had less .......... of the brain

text:

less deterioration

Paraphrase:

deterioration = decline / damage

Đáp án: deterioration

questions.text: Usually, smaller amounts of BDNF are found in the blood of people .......... with depression.
questions.question: Usually, smaller amounts of BDNF are found in the blood of people .......... with depression.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
depression
blood

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 4:

"people who have received a diagnosis of major depression"

Bước 3: Phân tích

question:

people ______ with depression

text:

received a diagnosis of depression

Paraphrase:

received a diagnosis = diagnosed

(Cần dạng quá khứ phân từ để rút gọn mệnh đề.)

Đáp án: diagnosed

questions.text: Exercise may lessen the effects of stress because it can .......... the effects of corticosteroids, which are produced by stress.
questions.question: Exercise may lessen the effects of stress because it can .......... the effects of corticosteroids, which are produced by stress.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
stress
corticosteroids

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn

Đoạn 4:

"Exercise can counteract this effect."

Bước 3: Phân tích

question:

lessen the effects of stress

text:

counteract this effect

Paraphrase:

counteract = reduce / offset / lessen the effect

Đáp án: counteract

questions.text: If you are suffering from a bad .......... that won't go away, it is important to see a doctor.
questions.question: If you are suffering from a bad .......... that won't go away, it is important to see a doctor.
questions.explanation:
Câu gốc: If you are suffering from a bad mood that won't go away, it is important to see a doctor.

Phân tích ngữ pháp: Chỗ trống đứng sau tính từ bad (tồi tệ) và cụm này nằm sau cấu trúc suffering from (chịu đựng cái gì). Theo quy tắc, sau tính từ bắt buộc phải là một Danh từ để tạo thành cụm danh từ.

Ý nghĩa & Loại trừ:

mood (Danh từ): Tâm trạng. Cụm "a bad mood" (một tâm trạng tồi tệ) là một cụm từ vô cùng phổ biến.

moodiness (Danh từ): Sự tính khí thất thường. Thường không dùng "a bad moodiness".

moody (Tính từ): Tính khí thất thường, buồn rầu. (Không đứng sau tính từ bad).

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.text: Mild depression may be a temporary response to the normal stresses of life, but ongoing depression could .......... a more serious condition.
questions.question: Mild depression may be a temporary response to the normal stresses of life, but ongoing depression could .......... a more serious condition.
questions.explanation: Phân tích ngữ pháp: Chỗ trống đứng ngay sau động từ khuyết thiếu (modal verb) could (có thể). Quy tắc bất di bất dịch trong tiếng Anh là sau các động từ khuyết thiếu (can, could, should, will, would, may, might...), chúng ta phải sử dụng một Động từ nguyên mẫu không "to" (Bare Infinitive).Ý nghĩa & Loại trừ:indicate (Động từ nguyên mẫu): Chỉ ra, là dấu hiệu của $\rightarrow$ Chọn.indication (Danh từ): Dấu hiệu, sự chỉ ra.indicative (Tính từ): Tỏ ra, ngụ ý (thường đi với "of").

questions.text: The doctor will ask you a series of questions and may recommend some tests to come up with a ...........
questions.question: The doctor will ask you a series of questions and may recommend some tests to come up with a ...........
questions.explanation: Phân tích ngữ pháp: Chỗ trống đứng sau mạo từ a trong cụm từ "come up with a..." (tìm ra/đưa ra một...). Sau mạo từ a/an/the, chúng ta bắt buộc phải điền một Danh từ đếm được số ít.Ý nghĩa & Loại trừ:diagnosis (Danh từ số ít): Sự chẩn đoán, lời chẩn đoán $\rightarrow$ Chọn (đưa ra một chẩn đoán).diagnose (Động từ): Chẩn đoán.diagnostic (Tính từ): Thuộc về chẩn đoán.

questions.text: If your condition is .........., the doctor may give you medication. If, on the other hand
questions.question: If your condition is .........., the doctor may give you medication. If, on the other hand
questions.explanation: Phân tích ngữ pháp: Chỗ trống đứng sau động từ liên kết (linking verb) is (to be). Sau động từ liên kết đóng vai trò bổ ngữ cho chủ ngữ "your condition" (tình trạng của bạn), chúng ta cần một Tính từ để chỉ tính chất, trạng thái của tình trạng đó.Ý nghĩa & Loại trừ:grave (Tính từ): Nghiêm trọng, trầm trọng $\rightarrow$ Chọn (Nếu tình trạng của bạn nghiêm trọng...).gravity (Danh từ): Trọng lực, hoặc độ nghiêm trọng. (Nếu dùng danh từ ở đây câu sẽ bị sai cấu trúc).gravely (Trạng từ): Một cách nghiêm trọng. (Trạng từ không làm bổ ngữ sau to be trong cấu trúc này).

questions.text: you are not suffering any serious disorder or .........., the doctor may recommend something as simple as regular exercise.
questions.question: you are not suffering any serious disorder or .........., the doctor may recommend something as simple as regular exercise.
questions.explanation: Phân tích ngữ pháp: Ở đây chúng ta gặp cấu trúc song hành với từ nối or (hoặc). Trước or là cụm "serious disorder" [Tính từ + Danh từ]. Do đó, để đảm bảo tính cân bằng về ngữ pháp, từ đứng sau or cũng phải là một Danh từ tương đương với "disorder".Ý nghĩa & Loại trừ:impairment (Danh từ): Sự suy giảm chức năng, sự hư hại $\rightarrow$ Chọn (rối loạn hoặc suy giảm chức năng).impair (Động từ): Làm suy yếu, làm hư hỏng.impaired (Tính từ/V3): Bị suy giảm, bị tổn thương.
